hypothesis: NN-BAX with a multi-fidelity surrogate that jointly learns from inexpensive LJ evaluations and a sparse set of high-fidelity DFT calculations will converge to the true MEP with at least a twofold reduction in the number of high-fidelity function evaluations required, while maintaining equivalent MAEi and fmax convergence criteria across LJ7, LJ38, and a representative small-molecule transition.
